svg.text: Top-to-bottom backend sketch: rename creates speculative state, schedulers execute out of order, ROB commits in order.
Inputs: decoded op, logical src/dst, immediate, branch metadata, ftq_id, exception bits.
Decode -> Rename pipeline register: uop fields, ftq_id, exception state, branch metadata
Eliminates WAW/WAR by mapping logical registers to physical registers.
Rename -> Dispatch pipeline register: renamed uops, ready bits, ROB index, branch mask, LSQ indexes
Writes renamed uops into the appropriate issue queues and marks operands ready if busy table says ready.
Dispatch -> Issue state update: IQ entries allocated; uops may now wait for wakeup/select
OOO issue: wakeup, ready CAM, select
CAM comparisons mark operands ready; select chooses ready uops for execution ports.
Issue -> Register Read pipeline register: selected uops, source physical tags, destination tags, FU port IDs
Physical register file ports and bypass network are major wide-core timing/area costs.
write PRF, clear busy bit, broadcast wakeup tag, mark ROB done
ROB keeps program order while execution completes out of order.
old physical dest returns to free list
take only when oldest at ROB head
uop has bit for each unresolved branch
clear bit when branch resolves correctly
kill uops when mispredicted bit matches
1. Rename map-table reads/writes + free-list allocation for wide rename.
2. Wakeup tag broadcast -> IQ CAM compare -> ready bits.
3. Select oldest ready uops per issue port.
4. PRF read ports + bypass muxes + FU input timing.
5. Writeback bus -> PRF write -> busy clear -> next wakeup.
Busy table tells whether physical source values are ready.
Issue queues hold the speculative dataflow graph.
ROB preserves program order for commit and precise exceptions.
Checkpoints/rollback restore speculative map and free-list state.
